size_correlation_finding.interpretation: Large mean/median gap reflects a skewed performance distribution: doing much better on some samples than others. Splitting by tampered-region size shows a real, meaningful difference (0.41 vs 0.19), consistent with ForensicsSAM's SAM-based architecture being naturally stronger at segmenting large, well-defined regions than subtle, small-area splices. However, weak linear correlation (0.056) indicates substantial additional variance beyond region size alone — this is a contributing factor, not the full explanation.

comparison_context: ForensicsSAM (mean F1=0.30) outperforms our own SupervisorV3 (F1≈0.24), Mantra-Net (best-threshold F1=0.077), and SPAN (best-threshold F1=0.076) on this dataset, consistent with its status as a 2025 foundation-model-based approach versus older or custom-trained architectures.
